title: Deyeuxia tenuis
intro: Deyeuxia tenuis, also known as Chinese feather grass, is a type of grass plant species native to China. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its purported health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce swelling and protect against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of Deyeuxia tenuis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, Deyeuxia tenuis is commonly prepared as a tea or infusion, or used in combination with other herbs to make a decoction, which is believed to promote relaxation and reduce inflammation.
taxonomy: Deyeuxia tenuis is classified within the family Poaceae, commonly known as grasses. It belongs to the tribe Poeae and is part of the genus Deyeuxia. The species is native to New Zealand and is known for its distinctive characteristics. Deyeuxia tenuis is further classified as Deyeuxia tenuis ( Hook.f.) Zotov.

It has been traditionally used in some regions to treat various health conditions, including fever and inflammation.
